name: Trinity-Large-Thinking
archDisplay: Sparse MoE, 256 experts (4 active + 1 shared, ~1.56% sparsity), sigmoid routing, SMEBU load balancing, interleaved local/global gated attention, depth-scaled sandwich norm
note: 398B-total/13B-active reasoning MoE pretrained from scratch on 17T tokens by Arcee AI, a small US startup (widely reported as building it 'from scratch to best Meta's Llama') — one of the largest open-weight models produced by an independent US lab. Sibling Trinity Mini (26B/3B active) and Trinity Nano (6B/1B active) were released alongside it but were not independently characterized here. BFCLv4 (70.1) and Tau2-Airline (88.0) scores were reported on the model card but omitted from results below because they don't cleanly match any existing benchmark slug (bfcl-v3 is a different/easier benchmark version, and no tau2-airline slug exists) — flagging bfcl-v4 as a plausible new slug to add rather than misfiling it under bfcl-v3.

results.notes: Agentic benchmark; #2 overall behind Anthropic Opus-4.6 (93.3) per press coverage

GDPval-AA v2 (Artificial Analysis's own real-world agentic-work-tasks eval, distinct taxonomy slug from plain 'gdpval') from AA's Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown, position-verified as the last (lowest-scoring) entry in the 28-model GDPval-AA v2 ranking list ('Trinity Large Thinking' = 3%). Not previously tracked; the file's own note already flags that BFCLv4 and Tau2-Airline were deliberately omitted for lacking a taxonomy slug, so this fills a genuine gap rather than duplicating that omission. [score corrected during round-6 completeness verify: proposed 3 -> 560]

Humanity's Last Exam score from Artificial Analysis's independent Intelligence Index v4.1 evaluation, position-verified against the full 28-model HLE ranking list ('Trinity Large Thinking' = 15%, second-to-last). AA's independent GPQA-Diamond re-run (75%) and IFBench re-run (56%) were checked but skipped since both slugs are already covered by the model's self-reported HF-card scores (76.3 and 52.3 respectively).

Tau2-Airline split, official HF model card (same source as the tracked tau2-bench-telecom=94.7 row). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

verificationNotes:
Architecture/training details from the Arcee Trinity Large technical report (arXiv:2602.17004); benchmark table and license from the official Hugging Face model card; pricing from Arcee's own docs site.

2026-07-16: Researched the 5 missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu). Checked the official HF model card (huggingface.co/arcee-ai/Trinity-Large-Thinking), Arcee's own docs (docs.arcee.ai/language-models/trinity-large-thinking, a verbatim mirror), the Trinity Large technical report (arXiv:2602.17004), and independent trackers (Artificial Analysis, Vals.ai, BenchLM.ai, AI Flash Report, OpenRouter). None publish a standalone MMLU, MATH-500, or HumanEval score for Trinity-Large-Thinking specifically. The technical report's MMLU=82.58/Minerva-MATH500=65.20/MBPP+=88.62 belong to the Trinity-Large-Base pretraining checkpoint (Table 3) and MMLU=87.21 to Trinity-Large-Preview (Table 4) — different post-training variants than Trinity-Large-Thinking, so per this corpus's known variant-conflation risk they are not carried over as this model's scores. MMMU is categorically inapplicable: this model is text-only (modalities=[text], capabilities.vision=false, no multimodal input support), and no vendor has run it on any vision benchmark. The HF card's 'Key Highlights' prose claims '98.2% on LiveCodeBench,' but this figure is uncorroborated and internally suspicious: it does not appear in the card's own benchmark comparison table (which has no LiveCodeBench column at all), it is numerically identical to GLM-5's Tau2-Telecom score in the adjacent table cell (suggesting a copy/paste error in the marketing bullet rather than a genuine measurement), Artificial Analysis's own structured data for this model records livecodebench:null, and BenchLM.ai — an independent aggregator that does track a dedicated Coding category for this model (SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-Bench Hard, SciCode) — lists no LiveCodeBench result at all. (aiflashreport.com's 'LiveCodeBench Reasoning: 33.0%' is a mislabeling of Artificial Analysis's unrelated 'AA-LCR' Long-Context-Reasoning metric, not a genuine LiveCodeBench score.) Given the lack of any independent corroboration and the specific internal inconsistency found, the 98.2% figure was judged unreliable and omitted rather than added. All five gaps resolved as omitted_no_evidence; no existing results were altered.

2026-07-18 re-chase of livecodebench + mmmu (round following 07-16 pass): independently re-verified both conclusions from scratch; no new evidence surfaced and both are reconfirmed as omitted_no_evidence.

LIVECODEBENCH: Re-checked all 6 method categories. (1) Official materials: re-fetched the HF card README directly — the "98.2% on LiveCodeBench" line is the ONLY mention of LiveCodeBench anywhere on the card, appears solely in the "Key Highlights" marketing-prose bullet (not in any benchmark table/column), and is absent from the full Arcee Trinity Large arXiv technical report (2602.17004, both v1 HTML and PDF checked directly for the string "LiveCodeBench" — zero hits; the report's actual eval tables cover MBPP+, Minerva-MATH500, MMLU, MMLU-Pro, GPQA-Diamond, SimpleQA, AIME25, none of them LiveCodeBench). (2) Independent trackers, freshly re-fetched: BenchLM.ai (has a dedicated Coding category with SWE-bench/AA-SciCode for this model) shows no LiveCodeBench entry; Artificial Analysis's own model page shows no LiveCodeBench score (only Intelligence Index=24, other categories listed with scores pending); vals.ai's model page shows only a CorpFin v2 entry, no LiveCodeBench; benchgecko.ai returned HTTP 503 (unreachable, not corroborating). aiflashreport.com again surfaces "LiveCodeBench Reasoning: 33.0%" — re-confirmed this is a mislabeled rendering of Artificial Analysis's unrelated "AA-LCR" (Long-Context-Reasoning) metric, not a real LiveCodeBench pass rate. (3) Academic: arXiv technical report checked directly (see above) — no LiveCodeBench mention for any Trinity variant. (4) GitHub: no Arcee or third-party eval-harness repo with LiveCodeBench run logs for this model found via targeted search. (5) Sibling-model comparisons: searched contemporaneous open-weight model launch/comparison writeups (DeepSeek, GLM, Kimi, Qwen coding roundups) for a citation of Trinity-Large-Thinking's LiveCodeBench score as a comparison point — none found; every web mention of the 98.2% figure traces back to the same single HF-card marketing bullet with no independent re-measurement. Conclusion unchanged: the figure is uncorroborated and internally inconsistent (absent from the card's own table, absent from the technical report, absent from every independent aggregator that separately tracks this model's coding scores), so it remains omitted rather than added.

MMMU: Reconfirmed architectural inapplicability. Corpus file itself states modalities=["text"] and capabilities.vision=false. Cross-checked Artificial Analysis's model page, which lists "MMMU-Pro" as one of its standard "Intelligence Evaluations" categories in its UI template but shows no score for Trinity-Large-Thinking specifically (consistent with AA being unable to run a vision benchmark on a text-only model) — this positively corroborates the inapplicability rather than just leaving it silent. No source (HF card, arXiv report, BenchLM, vals.ai, aiflashreport, VentureBeat coverage) mentions MMMU or any vision/multimodal result for this model. No vision variant of Trinity-Large-Thinking exists per any source checked.

No changes made to results[]; both gaps remain omitted_no_evidence with strengthened corroboration.
